title: [RELAX] Fix rotary embedding buffer size calculation
body:
- Change head_dim//2 to rotary_dim//2 in LongRope scaling
- Fixes buffer size when rotary_dim differs from head_dim when partial_rotary_factor < 1.0

step_plan.summary: Adjust LongRoPE rotary embedding scaling to size the `ext_factors` buffer by `rotary_dim//2` instead of `head_dim//2`, fixing shape mismatches when only a subset of head dimensions are rotated (e.g., partial rotary factor).

steps.operation: Fix LongRoPE ext_factors buffer sizing to use rotary_dim rather than head_dim in the generated TIR PrimFunc signature (and keep indexing consistent with rotary_dim).
steps.reason: When `partial_rotary_factor < 1.0`, `rotary_dim` can be smaller than `head_dim`, so declaring/allocating `ext_factors` as `(head_dim // 2,)` is incorrect for LongRoPE scaling (can cause shape mismatches or wasted allocation). The scaling factors are defined per rotated pair, i.e., `rotary_dim // 2`.
steps.side_effects: Changes the TIR PrimFunc parameter shape for the LongRoPE path, which may require any caller/allocation sites within this function (or downstream compilation artifacts) to pass an `ext_factors` tensor of length `rotary_dim//2`. May impact compile-time shape checks and any tests that assume the old buffer shape; add/update a regression test that exercises `rotary_dim != head_dim` under LongRoPE.
